name: git-workflows
description: Git workflows, branching strategies, commit conventions, CI/CD patterns, and collaboration practices

entries.title: Conventional Commits Standard
entries.content: Format: type(scope): description. Types: feat (new feature), fix (bug fix), docs (documentation), style (formatting), refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore. Breaking changes: feat!: or BREAKING CHANGE footer. Enables automated changelogs and semver.
entries.code:
# Feature
feat(auth): add Google OAuth login

# Bug fix
fix(api): handle null response from upstream

# Breaking change
feat(api)!: change pagination to cursor-based

BREAKING CHANGE: The `page` parameter is replaced with `cursor`.

# With body
fix(db): prevent connection pool exhaustion

The pool was not releasing connections on query errors.
Added proper try/finally blocks in all query functions.

# Scoped to component
refactor(ChatSidebar): extract message list to separate component

# CI/CD
ci: add Playwright E2E tests to GitHub Actions

# Dependencies
build(deps): upgrade Next.js to 15.5.12

entries.title: Branching Strategies (Trunk, GitFlow, GitHub Flow)
entries.content: Trunk-based: everyone commits to main, short-lived feature branches (<1 day), feature flags for WIP. GitHub Flow: main + feature branches, PR reviews, merge to main, deploy main. GitFlow: main, develop, feature/*, release/*, hotfix/* (complex, for scheduled releases).
entries.code:
# GitHub Flow (recommended for most teams)
# 1. Create feature branch from main
git checkout main && git pull
git checkout -b feat/add-search

# 2. Make commits
git add . && git commit -m "feat(search): add full-text search"

# 3. Push and create PR
git push -u origin feat/add-search
gh pr create --title "feat: add full-text search" --body "Adds search with debounce and highlights"

# 4. After review, merge (squash preferred for clean history)
gh pr merge --squash

# 5. Clean up
git checkout main && git pull
git branch -d feat/add-search

# Trunk-based (for experienced teams)
# Short-lived branches, merged within hours
git checkout -b fix/typo-header
git commit -am "fix: typo in header"
git push && gh pr create --title "fix: typo" && gh pr merge --squash

entries.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline
entries.content: Automate testing, building, and deployment with GitHub Actions. Run on push/PR. Matrix testing for multiple Node versions. Cache dependencies. Deploy to Vercel/AWS on main merge.
entries.code:
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18, 20]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm run build
      - run: npm test

  deploy:
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: amondnet/vercel-action@v25
        with:
          vercel-token: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}
          vercel-org-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}
          vercel-project-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_PROJECT_ID }}
          vercel-args: '--prod'

entries.title: Pull Request Best Practices
entries.content: Small PRs (< 400 lines). Descriptive title (conventional commit format). PR template with: what, why, how, testing. Link to issue. Self-review before requesting. Add screenshots for UI changes. Respond to all comments.
entries.code:
# .github/pull_request_template.md
## What
<!-- Brief description of changes -->

## Why
<!-- Link to issue, explain motivation -->
Closes #123

## How
<!-- Implementation approach, key decisions -->

## Testing
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Manual testing performed
- [ ] No console errors

## Screenshots
<!-- For UI changes, add before/after screenshots -->

## Checklist
- [ ] Code follows project conventions
- [ ] Self-reviewed the diff
- [ ] Types are correct
- [ ] No unnecessary console.log statements
- [ ] Documentation updated if needed
